summary: Frontend-only static HTML site (devashribuilders). Tested all 6 pages, interactive features (filters, lightbox, contact form validation, blog search, counters, navbar scroll, mobile drawer, newsletter). 12/13 features pass. 1 functional bug found: contact form success message never displays. 1 minor console error from React trying to mount on absent #root.

ui_bugs.issue: After valid form submission, '.form-success' success banner never displays. Root cause: in /app/frontend/public/js/main.js line 225, `form.querySelector('.form-success')` is used, but the .form-success div is rendered OUTSIDE the <form id='contact-form'> element in /app/frontend/public/contact.html, so the query returns null and .show class is never added. Form is reset() but user sees no confirmation.

Console pageerror on every page load: 'Target container is not a DOM element.' — CRA injects React bundle which calls createRoot(document.getElementById('root')) but the static index.html (and others) does not contain a #root div. App.js returns null but the error fires before that. Suggest adding
to public/index.html or guarding the mount in src/index.js.
Console warning: 'Scripts build/three.js and build/three.min.js are deprecated with r150+, and will be removed with r160. Please use ES Modules or alternatives.' Not breaking but worth migrating.

action_items:
FIX (HIGH): Either move
...
inside
...
in contact.html, OR change main.js line 225 from `form.querySelector('.form-success')` to `document.querySelector('.form-success')`. Currently the success banner never appears after a valid submit (form is cleared silently).

FIX (LOW): Add `
` to /app/frontend/public/index.html (and the other 5 html files) OR guard the React mount in src/index.js with `if (document.getElementById('root'))` to eliminate the page-load console error 'Target container is not a DOM element.'
OPTIONAL: Upgrade three.js usage from build/three.min.js to ES module import to avoid r160 removal.
critical_code_review_comments: main.js line 225 — querySelector scope assumption is wrong; the .form-success sibling lives outside the form element in contact.html. Use a stable selector or move the element inside the form.
src/index.js bundle mounts unconditionally on a non-existent #root, producing a noisy console error on every page navigation. Either remove the React bundle from the static pages or guard the mount.
Contact form: f.type === 'tel' regex /^[+\d\s\-()]{8,}$/ is acceptable but allows pure dashes/spaces; consider requiring at least 8 digits explicitly.

main.js puts the .invalid class on .form-group wrapper, but spec asked it on the input field. Visual behavior is OK (red border, error text) but the spec wording 'shows .invalid class on required fields' is satisfied at wrapper level, not input level — minor doc discrepancy.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (12/13 critical interactive features pass; contact success banner is the one failure)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 static pages load. Confirmed working: project filter chips (residential/commercial/luxury/all -> 5/3/3/11 cards), gallery filter chips + lightbox open/ESC-close/next, contact-form required-field validation (5 invalid on empty submit), contact-form email regex validation (red border + 'Please enter a valid email.'), blog search live filter, blog category chips, #blog-empty visibility on no-results, about.html counters animate 0->26/42/3,200/71 when scrolled into view, navbar 'scrolled' class on >0 scroll, scroll-progress bar width updates, .scroll-top.show appears, .nav-link.active matches current page, all 6 data-testid='nav-*' anchors resolve to correct .html href, mobile drawer .mobile-drawer.open + .drawer-overlay.open toggling via .hamburger and overlay-click close, footer newsletter form valid email -> 'Thanks — you'll hear from us soon.' Only failure: valid contact-form submit clears fields but .form-success stays display:none because element is outside form scope (see action_items).
rca_of_the_issue:
BUG: contact.html success banner not shown.
Reproduction: open /contact.html, fill all required fields with valid values (name, phone +919876543210, email john@example.com, city Pune, message text), click Send message. Expected: green .form-success banner becomes visible with 'Thank you. A planner will reach you within one working day.' Actual: form fields clear but no banner appears.
Root cause: /app/frontend/public/contact.html places <div class='form-success'> as a sibling AFTER </form>, not inside it. /app/frontend/public/js/main.js:225 calls `form.querySelector('.form-success')` which is scoped to descendants of the form and returns null. The subsequent classList.add('show') never executes.
Mitigation (one-line fix): change line 225 to `document.querySelector('.form-success')` OR move the .form-success div inside the form element in contact.html.
